one_line_description: Meta's text-based social network, with a Threads API for publishing posts, managing replies, and reading insights.
auth_summary: OAuth2 authorization code flow against Threads-specific endpoints: short-lived (1h) user access tokens exchangeable for long-lived 60-day tokens that must be refreshed before expiry; tokens carry Threads permissions (e.g. threads_basic, threads_content_publish).
access_notes: Create a Meta developer app with the Threads use case — self-serve in development mode (limited test users). Publishing for arbitrary users in production requires Meta App Review with screencast demos per permission (typically a few business days).
api_surface_summary: Graph-API-style REST endpoints for creating/publishing threads (text, image, video, carousel), reply management/moderation, user profile info, and post/account insights. No ads or DM surface.
main_blocker: Meta App Review required for production use with external users; 60-day token lifetime requires refresh handling
agent_notes: No official Threads MCP from Meta found — only community servers. Note one prominent GitHub result (quinnjr/threads-mcp) targets 'Threads.com', so vet community options carefully. Auth/token lifecycle confirmed from official docs snippets; I did not fetch the docs pages directly (session limit hit on that fetch).
